g.text: Saskatchewan’s rental markets entered 2025 with improving balance. Vacancy rates approached 3 %, reﬂecting
steady new completions and a cooling inﬂow of migrants. Rents plateaued after outsized 2023–24 gains, with most
urban landlords holding rates or o
ering small concessions. Construction activity remains healthy, supported by
government and CMHC ﬁnancing programs. A
ordability remains better than the national average, yet rising
rent-to-income ratios signal growing strain among lower-income tenants.
1101, 1121 109 St, North Battleford, Saskatchewan | File VAL251012 - 1
